source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that Service Account Tokens are only mounted where necessary

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that Service Account Tokens are only mounted where necessary
input_row.description: Service	accounts	tokens	should	not	be	mounted	in	pods	except	where	the	workload running	in	the	pod	explicitly	needs	to	communicate	with	the	API	server
input_row.rationale: Mounting	service	account	tokens	inside	pods	can	provide	an	avenue	for	privilege	escalation attacks	where	an	attacker	is	able	to	compromise	a	single	pod	in	the	cluster. Avoiding	mounting	these	tokens	removes	this	attack	avenue. Impact: Pods	mounted	without	service	account	tokens	will	not	be	able	to	communicate	with	the	API server,	except	where	the	resource	is	available	to	unauthenticated	principals.
input_row.audit: Review	pod	and	service	account	objects	in	the	cluster	and	ensure	that	the	option	below	is set,	unless	the	resource	explicitly	requires	this	access. automountServiceAccountToken: false
input_row.remediation: Modify	the	definition	of	pods	and	service	accounts	which	do	not	need	to	mount	service account	tokens	to	disable	it. Default	Value: By	default,	all	pods	get	a	service	account	token	mounted	in	them. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-service- account/ 212	|	P a g e CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 213	|	P a g e 5.2 Pod Security Policies A	Pod	Security	Policy	(PSP)	is	a	cluster-level	resource	that	controls	security	settings	for pods.	Your	cluster	may	have	multiple	PSPs.	You	can	query	PSPs	with	the	following command: kubectl get psp PodSecurityPolicies	are	used	in	conjunction	with	the	PodSecurityPolicy	admission controller	plugin.
input_row.impact: Pods	mounted	without	service	account	tokens	will	not	be	able	to	communicate	with	the	API server,	except	where	the	resource	is	available	to	unauthenticated	principals.
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-service- account/ 212	|	P a g e CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 213	|	P a g e 5.2 Pod Security Policies A	Pod	Security	Policy	(PSP)	is	a	cluster-level	resource	that	controls	security	settings	for pods.	Your	cluster	may	have	multiple	PSPs.	You	can	query	PSPs	with	the	following command: kubectl get psp PodSecurityPolicies	are	used	in	conjunction	with	the	PodSecurityPolicy	admission controller	plugin.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
Given the nature of the control and Kubernetes' architecture on Alibaba Cloud, this control can be verified programmatically by inspecting the pod and service account configurations within an Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) cluster. Alibaba Cloud APIs, such as those accessed via the Alibaba Cloud CLI or Kubernetes CLI tools like `kubectl`, can query the necessary configurations to determine if service account tokens are being unnecessarily mounted.

**DECISION CRITERIA:**
- Kubernetes API allows querying of pod and service account configurations, specifically inspecting the `automountServiceAccountToken` setting.
- The control refers to a standard configuration setting in Kubernetes that can be checked programmatically.
- Use of `kubectl` to review configurations is possible, which aligns with automated access methods.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ack_pod_service_account_token_mounting_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **API/CLI Tools:** Use `kubectl` to interact with the Kubernetes API to retrieve pod specifications and service account configurations.
- **Resource Enumeration:** Enumerate all pods and service accounts in the cluster.
- **Configuration Validation:**
  1. Use `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json` to retrieve pod configurations.
  2. Check each pod's specification for the field `automountServiceAccountToken`.
  3. Ensure that `automountServiceAccountToken` is either set to `false` or omitted when not needed by the application.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** Pod configurations have `automountServiceAccountToken: false` unless explicitly required.
  - **Fail:** Pods have `automountServiceAccountToken` enabled unnecessarily.
- **Expected Configuration:** 
  - Pods that do not explicitly need to communicate with the API server must have `automountServiceAccountToken` set to `false`.

This approach ensures that any unused service account tokens are not mounted, reducing the risk of privilege escalation attacks.
